name: create_support_case
description: Create a new AWS Support case with specified subject, service code, category code, severity code, and communication body. Use this when the user wants to open a new support ticket.

subject.description: The subject/title of the support case

service_code.description: The AWS service code (e.g., 'amazon-elastic-compute-cloud-linux' for EC2). Use describe_services to get valid codes.

category_code.description: The category code for the issue (e.g., 'instance-issue'). Use describe_services to get valid codes.

communication_body.description: The detailed description of the issue

cc_email_addresses.description: Optional list of email addresses to CC on the case

language.description: Language code (default: 'en' for English, 'zh' for Chinese)

attachment_set_id.description: Optional attachment set ID from add_attachments_to_set

name: describe_support_cases
description: Query and retrieve information about existing AWS Support cases. Can filter by case ID, date range, and resolution status. Use this to check on case status or search for cases.

case_id_list.description: Optional list of specific case IDs to retrieve

display_id.description: Optional display ID of a specific case (the case number shown in console)

after_time.description: Optional start date for filtering (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')

before_time.description: Optional end date for filtering (ISO 8601 format)

include_resolved_cases.description: Whether to include resolved/closed cases (default: false)

include_communications.description: Whether to include case communications/messages (default: true)

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (min: 10, max: 100)

next_token.description: Pagination token for retrieving next page of results

name: add_communication_to_case
description: Add a new communication/message to an existing support case. Use this to reply to AWS Support or provide additional information on an open case.

case_id.description: The internal case ID (not display ID). Use describe_support_cases to get the case_id from display_id.

cc_email_addresses.description: Optional list of email addresses to CC on this communication

attachment_set_id.description: Optional attachment set ID from add_attachments_to_set

name: resolve_support_case
description: Close/resolve an existing AWS Support case when the issue has been addressed. Use this when the user confirms the problem is solved.

case_id.description: The internal case ID to resolve. Use describe_support_cases to get the case_id from display_id.

name: describe_services
description: Retrieve the list of AWS services available for support cases, including their service codes, names, and valid category codes. Use this to find the correct service_code and category_code when creating a case.

service_code_list.description: Optional list of specific service codes to retrieve (e.g., ['amazon-elastic-compute-cloud-linux'])

name: describe_severity_levels
description: Retrieve the list of available severity levels for AWS Support cases with their codes and descriptions. Use this to understand severity options: low (general guidance), normal (system impaired), high (production impaired), urgent (production down), critical (business-critical down).

name: add_attachments_to_set
description: Upload one or more file attachments to a new or existing attachment set. Returns an attachment_set_id that can be used when creating a case or adding communication. Attachments must be base64-encoded and less than 5MB each. Attachment sets expire after 1 hour.

attachment_set_id.description: Optional ID of existing attachment set to add to (if not provided, creates new set)
